reviews.title: Not wheelchair friendly
reviews.text: Great hotel if you’re fit and healthy, forget it if you use a wheelchair, you can’t even get to the carpark without trekking through the neighbouring hotel. It’s almost like they have gone out of their way to deter people who are disabled.

reviews.title: Upscale hotel in need of refinement.
reviews.text:
Well-appointed rooms, and property, but unlikely to stay again. Resort fee does not include access to any of the spa facilities, and the gym and pool are both mediocre at best and don’t warrant such a fee. Access to the 

Signia is not worth the walk over, dirty pool and pool area in dire need of repairs.

reviews.title: From a Japanese perspective.
reviews.text:
Before booking, I couldn't find any reviews from Japanese people, so I will share my honest impressions from a Japanese perspective. The staff were friendly and kind, and the shuttle bus to Disney, including Springs, had frequent departures and arrived on time, making it very convenient. Additionally, the breakfast buffet was excellent in terms of taste, variety, and service. I saw online that there might be an additional charge for warm food, but we had a plan that included breakfast, and we were able to eat everything without any extra fees.

However, overall, I felt it was too expensive even considering the weak yen. Since it was our honeymoon, we splurged on a DX Queen room for over 80,000 yen per night (including breakfast), but using any facilities in the hotel (except for the pool) incurred extra charges, and of course, the snacks, coffee, and tea in the room were expensive as well. Despite that, the room was just normal even though it was spacious. The Aesop amenities were nice, but there was only one toothbrush, and there were no bathrobes. The walls were thin, and I could hear the conversations from the neighboring room. The cleaning was thorough, and we received enough bottled water for the number of guests every day, but I felt that was average, and this seemed to be included in the resort fee that we would pay later. I couldn't help but think that for the same amount of money spent in Japan, we could stay at a high-class ryokan with a private open-air bath and all-inclusive service...

Lastly, regarding the billing experience. When checking in, a deposit of $150 multiplied by the number of nights was temporarily held, and at checkout, it would be deducted based on the services used. This system is also unfamiliar in Japan, so if you’re anxious, I recommend getting a detailed bill at checkout. The resort fee of $50 per night is also deducted from this deposit at checkout. Since I stayed for 6 nights, it was over 50,000 yen… If resorts are like this, there’s nothing we can do, but I wish they would include this in the upfront pricing. I was surprised when I learned about this since I missed it, but it was clearly stated on Agoda's booking screen and the reservation confirmation document that the "Resort Fee is settled on-site."
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Room- 2 Queens- Mobility Acc W/tub

reviews.title: Good Stay
reviews.text: Too many hidden charges at checkout.  Parking fees, resort fees etc. The hotel is top notch and the staff is 5 star. Overall great stay.

reviews.title: great location
reviews.positives: great place

reviews.title: MEH...just so-so this time.
reviews.positives: Fireworks, connected rooms, valet?
reviews.negatives:
Not the Waldorf of which I am accustomed.

First day we arrived, our reservation (3 rooms) was screwed up. We used booking.com to reserve our stay. I took my time making sure that all the upgrades we wanted were accounted for on the website. When we arrived, the Waldorf advised that they did not have the rooms we requested even though they acknowledged early on that they received the request. They said there were no notes in their computer, but we received two confirmations. It too two hours to fix the mess bc the hotel is under renovation construction.

We came back two hours later, requested upper floor rooms but bc of the construction, they would not honor the request; we were placed on the 7th floor. We eventually got moved to our rooms. When we got settled, I set up to do some work and noticed that the dining chairs were all nasty. They had stains from previous occupants, which was so gross and not the calibre we would have ever expected from WAO. I called about it, someone was sent by the next day and said they would clean them or remove them and replace them w other chairs. We wanted to invite friends up but I never would have w chairs in the conditions in which they were. We just used hand towels to cover them when we needed to sit. There was also a tear in the rug that I could have easily trip on had I had on heels.

We went to shower and found there were no face towels so we had to call for those. They responded w/in 20 minutes, which we appreciated. We just wished they had been there prior to our arrival because it is a hassle to have to wait on towels when one wants to shower.

We got cleaned up and left for dinner. When we returned, we went to our rooms only to hear major construction noise. It. Was. HORRIBLE! I visited the front desk and was advised they would be done within the hour; remember though, I was placed on floor 7 to AVOID the noise. As we left to enjoy our time

The service staff were courteous and always smiley.

reviews.title: Fabulous luxury hideaway, amidst the magic
reviews.text: Amazing from the moment your through the door! Great staff, facilities, surroundings & comfortable. Breakfast, although not included with the room, was a joy each day! Coming back soon!!
reviews.positives: The pool, staff & setting.

reviews.title: EXCELLENT
reviews.text: 5 stars. As a frequent business traveler, the Waldorf Astoria Orlando delivered. Don’t be fooled by the Disney address—this is a top-tier hotel with polished service, quiet, well-appointed rooms, and genuinely professional staff. On-site restaurants are excellent (and convenient), meeting spaces run smoothly, and everything feels dialed in. Would absolutely return for work or a quick reset.

reviews.title: Disappointed with cleanliness of room.
reviews.positives: Nice lobby and pretty rooms. The zero entry pool was good for kids and was very warm. On site restaurant Aquamarine was in a good location relative to the pool. However, when booking, they should tell you that the pool with the lazy river is not at the Waldorf, but rather at a neighboring hotel with access by the Waldorf guests. That hotel was a hike on foot. Not convenient and we were annoyed that it wasn’t more transparent at time of booking.
reviews.negatives: Our blankets and sheets smelled horribly like mildew. Had an allergic reaction probably to the mildew from breathing it in all night. Didn’t sleep at all because of it.

reviews.title: Fantastic!
reviews.text: The Waldorf is a fantastic choice for families traveling with kids. It offers all the conveniences you could want—spacious accommodations, excellent amenities, and multiple kid-friendly pools. The dining options are top-notch, and getting to the Disney parks is quick and easy. The staff are warm and welcoming, making the whole experience even better. We’re already looking forward to our next stay!

reviews.title: Beautiful holiday & perfect for Disney
reviews.text: We stayed one night to be in position for Disney. The shuttle process is seamless. Hotel and rooms are lovely. We ate in peacock alley, which is a gorgeous bar area in the lobby & they even have a kids menu. Highly recommend the tuna tartare. I accidentally left my iPad there & they were helpful in getting that back to me in a timely manner. I would certainly stay there again.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: My go to hotel

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.text:
Thoroughly enjoyed my stay. Staff are courteous and kind. Good food choices, has a handy convenience store, delicious bakery that serves a variety of freshly baked goods by wonderful staff. 
Relaxed atmosphere, lazy river, cocktails, delicious food, and closer to Disney parks.

reviews.title: Great team,Great Hotel
reviews.text: The staff starting with CHad, the valet, were all excellent and very helpful. I cannot even begin to imagine the stress load the team here deals with on a daily, hourly, even minute basis. It's full during the day, more relaxed at night, but being Disney and a WA property, you can imagine there's always someone needing something. CHeckout with Armin was smoothe and efficient, and my check in with Shelby, along with dozens of others waiting, was quick and I was immediately given my room. It was the perfect place for me to relax overnight to catch a connecting flight the next day. These folks seem to try very hard and it shows, they were faultless. Oh, and the room etc was very well appointed and of good size. Athough, I thought it odd no mini bar? I don't drink, but for those who do...

reviews.title: Very Nice Hotel!
reviews.positives: Great Service, Big Rooms, Nice Pools

reviews.title: The best hotel in Orlando. Perfect location, food, and amenities.
reviews.positives: the room service food was absolutely incredible. and the front desk staff! Beloved was such a great hospitality professional.

reviews.title: Best staycation ever!
reviews.positives: Restaurants, room service dining, spa services.
reviews.negatives: I liked everything.

reviews.title: It was a peasant and memorable stay! Need to uplift their service level (maybe because the hotel was full when I went)
reviews.positives: Ambience, spacious, well designed, excellent toilet and very beautiful connected rooms with Kitchenette. Good for families. Nice programs. Very Good Coffee Shop. Excellent Golf Course.
reviews.negatives: Delay in attending for guest phone requests. Not good refills of amenities in the room, I had a problem with ants nearby the kitchenette.

reviews.title: Phenomenal Customer Service & Accommodations
reviews.positives: Your staff was exceptional! In previous years, I have stayed at the Ritz Carlton in Naples and the Four Seasons in Orlando but your staff went above and beyond to accommodate my family. We enjoyed your delicious room service daily served by polite staff members, we had a blast utilizing your beautiful pool cabanas with extraordinary service, and we spent our Thanksgiving dinner at the Bull & Bear restaurant that provided us with an extraordinary dining experience. Please keep up the excellent work! We will be back because your customer service is the best by far. Last but not least, I have to mention the incredible concierge team at your hotel. They texted me daily to answer any questions I may have, and made sure to offer their services until we departed. I left your hotel feeling well taken care of because of these remarkable individuals. Thank you! See you soon.
reviews.negatives: We had a lengthy wait at the Bull & Bear but we were a large party of ten on Thanksgiving evening. With that being said, I highly recommend providing larger parties with small children faster service to avoid any tantrums brought on by hunger or lack of beverages. Once we received our food, it was delicious and the dessert portion exceeded our expectations. Just keep in mind the needs of families enjoying a meal at your restaurant. Speedy service, and more kid friendly options would be greatly appreciated.
